big_picture.strategy: Two accounts, intentionally separated. @athena_hz = portfolio. @athena_huo = person behind it. Never duplicate. Cross-promote sparingly (one story mention per week max). Over time @athena_huo becomes the primary identity; @athena_hz becomes the portfolio to point at for paid work.
big_picture.only_overlap: Graduation day May 23 is the ONE day both accounts post about the same event — different versions: @athena_huo = emotional DITL reel, @athena_hz = polished lookbook.

athena_hz.purpose: Brand deal showcase. Where rate decks are sent. Where brands vet her.
athena_hz.posting_cadence: 1-2 posts per week, quality over quantity
athena_hz.status: KEEP AS IS during the transition. Do not rebuild while @athena_huo is launching.
do: Post 1-2 editorial-quality posts per week, no more
Continue UGC collaborations that fit existing aesthetic
Save graduation outfit for @athena_hz as a polished fashion post (the ONE overlap)

athena_huo.positioning: The last month of one life, the first month of another. Two-act show about a transition from Cornell to NYC, posted in real time.
athena_huo.purpose: Audience building — the real follower growth account.
athena_huo.audience: People who want to follow a person, not a brand
athena_huo.tone: Warm, observational, slightly literary, occasionally dry-funny
athena_huo.bio: cornell meng '26 · systems + software | ithaca → nyc this fall | life, taste, & the occasional spreadsheet | athenahuo.com
athena_huo.vibe: Ivy ambition × editorial taste × cozy slow-living × occasional dry wit
athena_huo.voice: Warm, observational, slightly literary, honest, quiet confidence
athena_huo.aesthetic: Warm parchment palette (cream, sienna, desaturated green). Film grain. No heavy filters. No bright pastels.

act_1.theme: The countdown era. Every post connects to one story: last month at Cornell.

pillars.name: Last Times
pillars.description: Countdown energy. Nostalgia. Specific Ithaca moments. 'things I'll miss.'

pillars.name: Aesthetic Daily Life
pillars.description: Morning routines, apartment, coffee, film shots, winery/park trips, study.

pillars.name: Graduation Prep
pillars.description: Outfit, nails, cap-and-gown, makeup, the whole runway.

pillars.name: The Moat
pillars.description: One post per 7-10 days. Engineer/builder/creative texture. NOT tech tutorials — subtle, woven in.

act_2.theme: The NYC era. Still one story: figuring out a new city and a new job.

pillars.name: Figuring It Out
pillars.description: Apartment hunt, moving, first subway, getting lost, new-city confusion.

pillars.name: First Job / Adult Girl
pillars.description: Vague-but-fancy. 'strategy/analytics at a tech company.' Coffee before office, walks home, sunday resets.

pillars.name: NYC Aesthetic
pillars.description: Cafes, outfits, walks, weekend life, neighborhoods.

phases_act_1.name: Phase 1: Establish the World
phases_act_1.focus: Introduce character (you), place (Ithaca), premise. Aesthetic daily life + occasional last-times.

phases_act_1.name: Phase 2: Open the Loop
phases_act_1.focus: Countdown references become explicit. Graduation prep starts. 1-2 posts hit above baseline reach.

phases_act_1.name: Phase 3: Land the Moment
phases_act_1.focus: Daily posting. Outfit reveals, cap-and-gown, senior week. Graduation reel is the hero.

structure.instruction: Face to camera. Deliver the opening hook. This is the whole game.
Face to camera or cut to b-roll. Setup line that makes the hook make sense.
B-roll of the day, voiceover over the top. Timestamps overlaid on beats.
Closing line (voiceover). Closing text overlay with countdown number.

voiceover_recording: Record AFTER shooting b-roll, not during
Voice memo app, quiet room, phone 6 inches from mouth
Slightly slower, slightly quieter than normal conversation
Natural pauses between sentences — gives editing room
Memorize the beats, not the exact words

rules: Face-to-camera only. No b-roll. No music. Natural light. 20-25 seconds.
One long thought. Not a montage. One coherent idea delivered with pauses.
Half-smile closer. Never grin. Observation, not performance.

must_do_one_of.example: I woke up at 9:48. 28 days before graduation.
The last month at Cornell is not what it looks like on Instagram.
I cried in the library last night.

ditl_examples: day one. the ordinary ones go the fastest. 29 to go.
day two. slow is a skill. 28 to go.
day three. collecting the small things. 27 to go.
caption_rules.bold_reveal_caption: 2-4 sentences. Reflective. No lists, no emojis, no CTA.
never: Teach or instruct (no '5 tips for X'). Documenting, not explaining.
Contain hashtags in caption (those go in first comment)

special.2026-05-23: today. (replaces number — the one post where the countdown breaks)

post_graduation.2026-05-24_to_2026-05-31: Bridge content — packing, final Ithaca moments
post_graduation.2026-06-01_to_2026-06-06: Moving week — apartment arrival, first NYC days
post_graduation.2026-06-07_onward: New countdown: 'week 1 in nyc.' Or new framing: 'city 1, month 1.'

lighting: Window light on the SIDE of face, not straight on
If indoor, one warm lamp > overhead lights

editing_reels.color_grade: saturation -10 to -15, warmth +5 to +7, shadows slightly lifted
editing_reels.audio_mix: Background music 10-20% so voiceover/ambient always priority

editing_photos.film_grain: VSCO A6 or A4, dialed back 30-40%

aesthetic.film_camera: Differentiator. Film photos get 2-3x saves of digital. Aim for 1-2 film photos per week.
aesthetic.grid_cohesion: Plan 9 posts ahead. Alternate textures: face → object → landscape. Avoid two of the same type side by side.

trending_audio_april_2026.title: Someday We Will Dream About Today
Ray Mysterio
trending_audio_april_2026.note: Cinematic, makes rough footage look intentional

hashtags.rule: Always in first comment, never in caption. Exactly 5 tags. 2 niche + 2 mid + 1 broad.

priority_order.note: Signals content worth returning to. Highest algorithmic weight 2026.
Signals worth sending to someone. Second highest.
First 2 seconds decide distribution. Completion to end = more push.
Interest in YOU, not just the post.
The conversion metric that matters most.
Vanity metric. Barely matters for distribution.

growth_targets_athena_huo.end_of_june_1mo_nyc: 5,000 followers, consistent 3k+ views/reel, first brand DM
growth_targets_athena_huo.end_of_august: 10,000 followers, reliable engagement, first paid brand deal

weekly_review_sunday: Top-performing post — what made it work

red_flags.views_declining_wow: Reassess format — usually too much variety, not enough consistency
red_flags.followers_up_engagement_down: Audience misaligned — bold reveal content attracting wrong people
red_flags.saves_under_1pct_of_views: Not save-worthy — need more specificity or reflective content
red_flags.follows_stalling_under_1k_for_6_weeks: Positioning not hitting — rework hooks, try sharper reveals

say: operational analyst at a tech company
strategy/analytics at a tech company in nyc

what_she_did_right: Picked ONE identity ('IB in SF') and hammered it
Used most searched/envied job on internet as her niche
Strong hooks — every reel opens with a clear contradiction or claim
Visual consistency — similar thumbnails, similar body of work
what_we_stole: One identity hammered: 'last month at cornell → moving to nyc'
Sharp hook structure for every post
Visual consistency through voiceover DITL format
Countdown mechanic — every post has a reason to exist within the larger arc
what_we_did_differently: Built-in acts — hers plateaus when she's no longer an IB analyst. Ours has act 2 (NYC) and act 3 (first year) ready.
Don't rely on shock-reveal alone (lower brand deal CPM)
Preserve @athena_hz's separate positioning for higher-paying fashion deals

top_5_remember: One story. last month at cornell → nyc. every post connects.
One format. voiceover DITL with 4-part structure. 1 bold reveal per week max.
Sharp hooks. numbers, contradictions, or uncomfortable truths. never 'people keep asking.'
Short captions. document, don't explain. day + observation + countdown.
Countdown always. every post ends with the number. it's the series signature.
top_5_avoid: Format variety for its own sake — consistency wins
Long captions that teach or instruct — wrong account
Posting about the company name or specific job details
Copying sw_v01's shock-reveal style too hard — wrong CPM ceiling
